title: Domain Physics Schema V1
description: JSON Schema for Project Lumina Domain Physics documents (derived from YAML source).

id.description: Globally unique domain identifier. Format: domain/{org}/{name}/v{major}

domain_authority.description: The human Domain Authority who authored this domain pack

meta_authority_id.description: ID of the domain pack one level above, or 'root' for top-level packs

lumina_core_version.description: Version of lumina-core-v1 this pack targets

invariants.description: List of domain invariants. Must include at least one critical invariant.

standing_orders.description: List of bounded automated responses the orchestrator may take

escalation_triggers.description: Conditions that cause the system to escalate to the Meta Authority

artifacts.description: Domain artifacts (achievements, milestones) that can be earned in this domain

subsystem_configs.description: Optional subsystem configuration blocks, keyed by subsystem ID. Each value is a free-form object of parameters specific to that subsystem (e.g. {"zpd_monitor": {"min_challenge": 0.3, ...}} for education, or {"soil_health_monitor": {...}} for agriculture). Omit entirely for domains that use no configurable subsystems.

path.description: Relative path from the domain module directory to the evidence-schema.json file.

version.description: Version of the evidence schema. Must match the 'version' field in the referenced evidence-schema.json.

evidence_schema.description: Reference to the domain module's evidence schema declaration file. See standards/domain-evidence-extension-v1.md for the Domain Evidence Extension standard.

tool_adapters.description: List of tool adapter IDs available in this domain

glossary.description: Domain vocabulary definitions. Students may query these terms during a session; the system performs server-side lookup and returns the definition without penalizing off-task ratio.

permissions.description: chmod-style permission block controlling read, write, and execute access to this module. See rbac-permission-schema-v1.json for the full schema.

enabled.description: When true, users with the guest role may access this module according to the permissions string. When false or omitted, guests are denied all access.

permissions.description: Permission characters granted to guest users: r (read) and/or x (execute). Guests may never write.

guest_access.description: Optional domain-scoped access policy for the guest role (RBAC Level 4). Each domain opts in independently. Omit entirely or set enabled=false to deny guest access.

enabled.description: When true, the document ingestion pipeline is available for this module.

max_file_size_mb.description: Maximum upload file size in megabytes.

accepted_formats.description: Document formats accepted by the ingestion pipeline.

max_interpretations.description: Maximum number of SLM interpretation variants to generate for ambiguous content.

ingestion_config.description: Configuration for the document ingestion pipeline. Controls which formats are accepted, size limits, and SLM interpretation parameters.

enabled.description: When true, this domain participates in cross-domain synthesis analysis during night cycle processing. When false or omitted, the domain remains fully isolated.

peer_domains.description: Domain IDs this domain allows synthesis comparison with. Both domains in a pair must list each other for analysis to proceed (mutual opt-in). Empty array or omitted means no peers.

share_glossary.description: When true, glossary terms are included in the structural comparison pass.

share_invariant_structure.description: When true, invariant structural patterns are included in the physics comparison pass.

cross_domain_synthesis.description: Opt-in configuration for cross-domain synthesis. Enables controlled bridging between otherwise isolated domain VLANs to identify structural and invariant similarities. Omit entirely or set enabled=false to keep this domain fully isolated. Both domains in a pair must mutually opt in.

enabled.description: When true, the logic scraping tool is available for this domain. Only domain authorities may trigger scrapes.

max_iterations.description: Maximum number of LLM iterations per scrape invocation.

synthesis_yield_threshold.description: Minimum novel synthesis yield rate (flagged / total iterations) below which the scrape is considered low-value.

feedback_mode.description: How prior responses are fed back to the LLM. cumulative: all prior responses included. sliding_window: only the last N responses included.

sliding_window_size.description: Number of prior responses to include when feedback_mode is sliding_window.

logic_scraping.description: Configuration for the logic scraping tool. Enables iterative LLM probing for novel synthesis discovery. Domain authorities invoke scrapes on-demand; flagged results are escalated for review via the novel synthesis two-key verification gate.

schema_version.description: Version of the domain role schema in use.

roles.description: Domain-specific role definitions ordered by hierarchy_level. The Domain Authority is implicitly level 0 and must not be listed here.

domain_role.description: Domain-scoped role ID receiving the access grant. Must match a role_id in the roles array.

scope.description: Optional scope qualifier limiting what the access grant applies to. Values are domain-defined (e.g., session_monitoring, ctl_records_own_students).

role_acl.description: Extended ACL entries for domain roles. Provides scoped permission overrides beyond the default_access declared on each role definition.

domain_roles.description: Optional domain-scoped role hierarchy. Defines roles that exist only within this domain, beneath the Domain Authority ceiling. Each domain can define its own role tiers (e.g., teacher/teaching-assistant/student for education, site-manager/field-operator/observer for agriculture). The Domain Authority is always the implicit ceiling at hierarchy level 0. Omit entirely for domains that use only the 7 system-level roles.

id.description: Unique identifier for this invariant within the domain

severity.description: critical: halts autonomous action; warning: triggers a standing order

check.description: Predicate expression evaluated by the orchestrator (e.g. 'step_count >= 3'). Required when handled_by is absent; optional (informational) when handled_by is set.

handled_by.description: ID of the subsystem that evaluates this invariant (e.g. 'zpd_monitor' for education, 'soil_health_monitor' for agriculture). When present, the orchestrator skips its own check evaluation and defers entirely to the named subsystem. Enables domain-agnostic invariant delegation without hardcoding invariant IDs in the engine.

standing_order_on_violation.description: ID of the standing order to invoke when this invariant is violated

signal_type.description: Optional signal type emitted when this invariant fires (e.g., NOVEL_PATTERN)

id.description: Unique identifier for this standing order

action.description: The automated action taken (e.g., request_more_steps, reduce_challenge). Domain-specific action names such as zpd_scaffold are defined by the domain pack and passed through as-is by the engine.

trigger_condition.description: Invariant ID or drift condition that activates this order

max_attempts.description: Maximum times this order may be applied before escalation

escalation_on_exhaust.description: Whether to escalate when max_attempts is reached

description.description: Human-readable description of what this standing order does

target_role.description: Role of the Meta Authority receiving this escalation

sla_minutes.description: SLA for acknowledgement in minutes; breach is itself a reportable event

unlock_condition.description: Description of mastery conditions required to earn this artifact

skills_required.description: Skill IDs that must meet the mastery threshold

term.description: Canonical term (lowercase). The primary lookup key.

definition.description: Concise, student-appropriate definition of the term.

aliases.description: Alternate forms a student might use (plurals, colloquial phrasings).

related_terms.description: Other glossary terms to surface after the definition.

example_in_context.description: A concrete example using the term in a domain-relevant sentence.
